content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN consisting only of characters 000 and 111. You are allowed to perform at most KKK operations on this string. In each operation, you can choose an index iii (where 0≤i<N−10 \le i < N-10≤i<N−1) and update the character at position iii as follows:

Si=max⁡(Si,Si+1)S_i = \max(S_i, S_{i+1})Si​=max(Si​,Si+1​)Here, the maximum is taken in the numerical sense, i.e., ′1′'1'′1′ is considered greater than ′0′'0'′0′. This operation essentially means that if Si+1S_{i+1}Si+1​ is ′1′'1'′1′, then SiS_iSi​ becomes ′1′'1'′1′ regardless of its previous value.

Your task is to determine the maximum possible number of 111s in the string after performing at most KKK such operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains a binary string SSS of length NNN, consisting of the characters 000 and 111.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of 111s that can be achieved in the string after performing at most KKK operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: You can apply the operation at index i=1i = 1i=1 and the string changes to "111". Maximum possible ones = 3.
Test Case 2: You can apply the operation at index i=1i = 1i=1 and the string changes to "011". Maximum possible ones = 2.
Test Case 3: You can apply the operation at index i=1i = 1i=1 and the string changes to "011". The next operation will be applied at index i=0i = 0i=0 and the string changes to "111". Maximum possible ones = 3.
